title: Ensure nodev option set on /var/log partition

prose.en: Ensure nodev option set on /var/log partition

description.en: The `nodev` mount option specifies that the filesystem cannot contain special devices.

rationale.en: Since the `/var/log` filesystem is not intended to support devices, set this option to ensure that users cannot create a block or character special devices in `/var/log`.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/var/log`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `nodev` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/var/log` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/var/log` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /var/log
```

```
 /var/log         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/var/log`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `nodev` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/var/log` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/var/log` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /var/log
```

```
 /var/log         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

references: See the fstab(5) manual page for more information.
